title: Real EvaStore in Harajuku
wikitext:
[]: Real EvaStore in Harajuku             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 08, 2011 10:25 am http://www.evastore.jp/real/index.html

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Jul 08, 2011 10:31 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 08, 2011 10:34 am http://www.evageeks.org/2011/07/evangelion-store-real-in-harajuku-tokyo/ It just says that it'll be open sometime in the Fall, there will be some unique items for sale, and that they'll be holding some events there.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Jul 08, 2011 10:42 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Jul 08, 2011 11:02 am Will this store also be online, or even in English?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Jul 08, 2011 11:41 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Will this store also be online, or even in English?

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Mon Jul 11, 2011 4:09 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Well, the Evangelion Store is already online (this is supposed to be the real/physical version of the online one); regarding the second question, Gainax stopped caring about gaijin years ago, when they stopped taking orders from outside Japan (apparently because of many frauds), I don't know if they'll ever reverse their decision.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jul 11, 2011 4:41 am No, I was referring to oversea shipping, back in the days the Gainax Store accepted international orders, that was the main reason for the English version of their website. Actually I don't remember when they stopped accepting orders, anyway they kept a non-updated English version for quite some time before getting rid of it (unfortunately) after a major restyling of their website. EDIT: they stopped accepting international orders from 31/01/2003 , I think they removed the English pages in 2006, there aren't crawled pages after 2005.

[]: New EVA store in Tokyo             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Mon Nov 21, 2011 8:38 pm Check this out, opened on Monday in Tokyo: [url]http://www.alafista.com/2011/11/21/evangelion-store-tokyo-01-grand-opening/[/url] [url]http://blog.evacustom.jp/post/13108266765/tokyo01[/url] Now a convenient one-stop shop for all of your Evangelion needs.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Nov 22, 2011 2:43 pm NewType with some coverage: http://anime.webnt.jp/report/view/2769 Radio EVA's coverage (interesting silhouette down at the bottom) http://radio-eva.jp/blog/2011/1122-5572.php Some of the unique items at the Harajuku store: http://www.evastore.jp/real/index.html#item That ring is kind of neat.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Nov 22, 2011 7:28 pm Kaji's cabbage field? They even have fusion cuisine for Eva http://radio-eva.jp/evameshi/ According to reports I read, the idea is to build Harajuku into OTAKU-03 (after Akihabara and Nakano) :) And Natsuki Katoh, otaku model+actress, looks so gorgeous - she may not look so much like Misato, but she is as attractive if not more :)

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Wed Nov 23, 2011 9:16 pm How I would love to go to one of these stores. It would be so much fun just to browse. Too bad some of the stuff is obnoxiously expensive though. Are Japanese shirts usually like, $70 or are people just paying an Eva tax?
